programme.desc: Jill (Patricia Richardson) accuses Tim (Tim Allen) of being insensitive after he ruins an honorary dinner with the library committee.

programme.desc: Balking at promoting a questionable product jeopardizes Tim's job.

programme.desc: Tim and Jill (Tim Allen, Patricia Richardson) have different plans for a monetary gift the boys have received. With Richard Karn.

programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) wants to add a power-driven snowstorm to the Taylor's Christmas-card family photo. With Patricia Richardson.

programme.desc: Jill (Patricia Richardson) cannot understand why Tim (Tim Allen) encourages Randy (Jonathan Taylor Thomas) to enter a grudge-match sled race.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: The Formans discover Jackie has been sleeping at their place because she has nowhere else to go; Fez realizes Nina is reluctant to introduce him to her family.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric must return Donna's engagement ring after he receives a collection notice.

programme.title: That '70s Show

programme.title: Ready for a safer shower? Enjoy a one-day bath remodel with Jacuzzi.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: It's one problem after another when Tim and Al go ice fishing.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: When Jill's (Patricia Richardson) retired father (M. Emmet Walsh) arrives for a visit, the house is transformed into a small military base.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) decides to separate Randy and Brad (Jonathan Taylor Thomas, Zachery Ty Bryan) when their arguing becomes too loud for his liking.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim and Al (Tim Allen, Richard Karn) each head up a Habitat for Humanity house-building project. Former President Jimmy Carter guest stars as himself.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: A role reversal occurs when Tim and Al host a cooking show, with Al as the star and Tim as the sidekick.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.sub-title: It Was the Best of Tims, It Was the Worst of Tims

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: A shortage of funds puts an end to Tim's (Tim Allen) plans for a special edition of "Tool Time." With Richard Karn.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim's (Tim Allen) Mother's Day plans for Jill (Patricia Richardson) go awry when he gives away a nostalgic swing set. Guest star: Victoria Principal.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Ben and Darlene are excited to share their good news with the family, but Neville, Jackie, Aldo and Harris share some very similar news of their own.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.sub-title: A Judge and a Priest Walk Into a Living Room...
programme.desc: The Conners gear up for a megawedding; Jackie and Neville, Darlene and Ben and Harris and Aldo are planning for a shared big day, but along the way, they discover that some of the couples may not be on the same page about their happily ever afters.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Cheryl tries to create a relaxed, low-pressure environment for Jim after he develops high blood pressure.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: A night on the town turns into a disaster for Cheryl and Jim; Dana cajoles Andy into doing her delinquent taxes.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Vanessa learns a secret that she thinks will ruin Ed and Bonnie's wedding day.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike and Vanessa each try to influence Jen's career path; Kristin uses her pregnancy to her advantage with Outdoor Man customers.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Vanessa turns to Mike and Carol for advice on her State Assembly campaign; Kyle is overcome with anxiety when Ed gifts him an expensive watch.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike's offer to help Jen with her school's bake sale goes up in smoke; Kyle finds unlikely advice from Joe on where he should enroll in college.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: Adam gets jealous when Teddy names Andi as his hero for a school essay.

programme.title: Man With a Plan
programme.desc: When Adam accidentally invites Andi to go on the guys' annual trip, he tries to uninvite her, which only makes matters worse.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: A chance meeting in the supermarket lets Jim in on Cheryl's secret: though she says the doctor told her to stay in bed, her doctor actually wants her to be active.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.sub-title: The Devil Went Down to Oak Park
programme.desc: The devil (James Lipton) comes to collect on a deal Jim made with him back when Jim was courting Cheryl.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim guilts the older kids into doing household chores after Jonathan's special blanket goes missing.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: When Dana, Cheryl's sister and best friend, moves away, Jim realizes he must find Cheryl a new friend as soon as possible.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.sub-title: What You See Is What You Get

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Randy, pretending to be thirty-two years old, meets a woman on a singles message board on the computer, and she comes by for a visit; Tim tries to help Mark with his science project.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) and Bob Vila face off in a handyman auction and also compete in an auto race. With Bob Vila as himself.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.sub-title: Back in the Saddle Shoes Again

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim takes over housekeeping duties to give Cheryl time to grieve when her favorite uncle dies, but begins to suspect Cheryl is faking her grief to get him to do more housework.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim, Ryan and Andy learn that Cheryl and Dana have appeared in a "Chicks Gone Wild" video.

programme.title: Major League
programme.desc: Lackluster baseball players (Tom Berenger, Charlie Sheen, Corbin Bernsen) hear their Cleveland team's new owner is counting on them to lose.
